entity.name: Morne
entity.description: I'm a self-taught developer building open-source accessibility tooling for Windows. I have physical challenges with my hands, which is the reason Samsara exists: I needed a fast, reliable voice-control and dictation layer to keep working, and built one. I care about tools that give people back the ability to use their computers on their own terms, and I release my work as open source so others facing similar barriers can use and extend it freely.

projects.name: Samsara
projects.description: Samsara is an open-source voice control and dictation application for Windows, built for people who cannot rely on a keyboard and mouse. It provides wake-word activation, hands-free dictation with pause/resume, and an extensible plugin system (window switching, web shortcuts, find-on-page, Discord integration, and more). It runs entirely on the user's own machine, uses a modular state-machine architecture, and ships with a CI pipeline and unit tests. It is actively developed and designed to be a daily-driver accessibility tool rather than a demo.

plans.name: Part-time developer support
plans.description: Covers part-time development to expand Samsara's accessibility features, improve wake-word reliability, harden the plugin system, and support users who depend on it daily.

plans.name: Goodwill
plans.description: Contribute any amount to support ongoing development.
